title: Post-earthquake glass cleanup boundary
summary: A safe boundary for broken glass after an earthquake, aftershock, shelf collapse, window failure, or shop damage when shoes, pets, children, and darkness increase injury risk.
content:
A safe boundary for broken glass after an earthquake, aftershock, shelf collapse, window failure, or shop damage when shoes, pets, children, and darkness increase injury risk.

This card improves post-earthquake glass cleanup boundary by making the action small, visible, and reversible. It helps a tired group avoid hidden assumptions while preserving emergency-service priority.

Core sequence: 1) Put shoes on everyone before any cleanup, including people who were asleep. 2) Move children and pets to a glass-free room or marked area before sweeping. 3) Use cardboard, stiff paper, or a dustpan from the edge inward; do not pick invisible shards with bare fingers. 4) Mark the cleaned path as passable but continue checking feet, paws, bedding, bags, and food surfaces.

Stop point: Do not use post-earthquake glass cleanup boundary to bypass emergency responders, utility professionals, medical advice, or evacuation instructions.
materials: stiff cardboard glass scoop prepared specifically for Post-earthquake glass cleanup boundary
dated owner note for Post-earthquake glass cleanup boundary
separate safe place linked to Earthquake · Glass cleanup
visible redirection mark for Post-earthquake glass cleanup boundary
steps: Put shoes on everyone before any cleanup, including people who were asleep.
Move children and pets to a glass-free room or marked area before sweeping.
Use cardboard, stiff paper, or a dustpan from the edge inward; do not pick invisible shards with bare fingers.
Mark the cleaned path as passable but continue checking feet, paws, bedding, bags, and food surfaces.
warnings: Do not continue post-earthquake glass cleanup boundary if the action increases fire, fumes, contamination, injury, violence, or official-order risk.
Do not leave post-earthquake glass cleanup boundary dependent on a single tired person without a written handover.
common_mistakes: Using post-earthquake glass cleanup boundary without checking the weakest person first makes the routine look complete while help is still missing.
Changing post-earthquake glass cleanup boundary verbally without changing the visible note creates two competing versions.
alternatives: If post-earthquake glass cleanup boundary cannot be done as planned, reduce it to a marked safe area, one named owner, and one next review.
If equipment for post-earthquake glass cleanup boundary is missing, use observation, distance, labeling, and official help instead of improvised high-risk repair.
failure_signs: The visible note for post-earthquake glass cleanup boundary no longer matches what people are actually doing.
Someone says they assumed post-earthquake glass cleanup boundary was handled, but no time, owner, or result is written.
when_not_to_use: Do not use post-earthquake glass cleanup boundary to bypass emergency responders, utility professionals, medical advice, or evacuation instructions.
Do not continue post-earthquake glass cleanup boundary inside smoke, floodwater, sewage, unstable walls, or a location with suspected gas or electrical danger.
short_term: Before moving on, make the first result of post-earthquake glass cleanup boundary visible and confirm one person can explain it in under thirty seconds.
long_term: After real use, improve post-earthquake glass cleanup boundary by replacing unclear wording, weak storage choices, and unsafe shortcuts with local practice notes.
if_method_fails: If post-earthquake glass cleanup boundary fails, mark the unsafe part unavailable, retreat to the last safe option, and request official or qualified support when needed.
environment_notes: Adapt post-earthquake glass cleanup boundary to apartment blocks, farms, islands, camps, shops, ferries, heat, smoke, low light, noise, and mobility limits.
